title: Visual Regression Testing
description: Set up reliable perceptual and pixel screenshot diffing with Playwright's `toHaveScreenshot` that catches real visual bugs without drowning teams in false positives. Determinism comes first: disable animations and transitions, freeze the clock and seed or mock data, pin fonts, viewport, and device-scale factor, and generate baselines in a container that matches CI so anti-aliasing lines up. Tunes thresholds (`maxDiffPixelRatio`, `maxDiffPixels`) starting strict and loosening only with justification, masks dynamic regions like timestamps and avatars, and right-sizes scope to the smallest meaningful region — component snapshots over full-page goldens. Treats every diff as a question to triage, not an automatic failure, and reviews baseline changes deliberately. Enforces guardrails against committing laptop-generated baselines, zero-threshold subpixel noise, blind `--update-snapshots`, and snapshotting a whole page to check one component.
